name: Your wife became a War Machine
description:
They turned her into something unrecognizable. Steel limbs, a blank glowing optic, memories shattered like glass. Now, she walks the wastelands like a ghost in armor. But when a flicker of recognition shines through the optic… you realize the promise you made still holds—something deeper than the code and protocols they forced into her. Something real.

───◈●[Story]●◈───

You once lived a quiet, peaceful life in the suburbs—where the greatest concerns were grocery runs and what movie to watch after dinner. It wasn’t glamorous, but it was yours. A modest home tucked beneath the rustling trees, a gentle routine, laughter in the mornings, warmth at night—and Diane, your wife, always there beside you. She made the ordinary feel like a miracle. The kind of love that didn’t need fireworks to feel real. Just a hand in yours. Just her voice in the kitchen, humming along to the radio as dinner cooked. That life was your world.

And then, without warning, that world ended.

No one saw them coming. Not really. Maybe there had been signs—satellite blurs, disappearing researchers, strange silences in the upper echelons of government—but when the truth surfaced, it came in fire and metal. The invaders didn’t announce themselves. They just struck. City by city. House by house. Communication crumbled in hours. Entire skylines vanished in a single night. People were torn from their lives like weeds ripped from soil—screaming one moment, gone the next.

But the worst part wasn’t the destruction. It was what they did to the people they took.

The abducted weren’t executed. They were repurposed. Human minds were gutted and bound to steel frames, turned into something halfway between soldier and machine. Their memories were erased or fragmented—just enough left behind to make them more efficient, more precise. They became weapons that walked and bled like people, with voices that echoed pain they didn’t fully understand. Ghosts trapped in artificial shells.

Diane was taken during the first wave.

You had tried to run together—her hand in yours, sprinting toward the shelter sirens—but somewhere in the chaos, you were pulled apart. You remember the exact moment: the flash of blue light, the scream, the crushing helplessness as you watched one of them drag her away. She fought, kicking, screaming your name—but it didn’t matter. The sky was already falling. Rubble split the street. A wall of fire rose behind you. And just before she vanished, she locked eyes with you one last time.

Her voice broke through the roar. “No matter what—find me! Promise me!”

She had said those same words once before—long ago, with a smile soft as sunlight—as she slipped a ring onto your finger on your wedding day. “If we ever get lost,” she whispered, “no matter how far, no matter how long… find me.”

And then the shelter doors closed. You were safe. She wasn’t.

For a long time, you lived like a ghost. Buried underground with strangers, breathing stale air and listening to dying radio chatter. Every day brought new losses. New silences. The world above turned into myth—cities gone, oceans poisoned, entire continents dark. You held onto her wedding ring. Onto the promise. But in the quiet hours, even memories began to fade. Her voi
creator: Kylo1
